title: calamintha acinos var. villosa
intro: Calamintha acinos var. villosa, also known as mountain basil or hairy calamintha, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, pink or purple flowers and hairy leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of Calamintha acinos var. villosa include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. In herbal preparations, it is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be added to salads and other dishes for its unique flavor and aroma.
